page: Microsoft Azure D8ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadv6 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical AMD EPYC 9V74 cores running at 3.7 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration (AES, VAES, SHA_NI). The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes 472 GB of local NVMe storage with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance places this instance in the top tier (top 10%), and its Geekbench single-core score is in the top 25%. Text processing performance is also highly competitive, ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, such as read-only small block (607.6 GB/sec) and large block (38.94 GB/sec), also align with average tier performance. For language model workloads, the instance demonstrates strong performance for small model text generation (135M model at 257.04 tokens/sec) but exhibits weak performance during prompt processing on medium models (7B model at 23.56 tokens/sec).

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D8ads_v6 offers a balanced resource density, pairing dedicated AMD EPYC compute with a substantial 472 GB local disk. This configuration avoids the premium costs associated with specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware while delivering high single-threaded performance. The instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, text processing applications, database operations, static web serving, and lightweight LLM text generation. However, it is less optimal for heavy multi-threaded compute or large-scale model prompt processing due to its average multi-core scaling and weak medium-model prompt processing benchmarks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D8ads_v6 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs from 4 physical AMD EPYC 9V74 cores clocked at 3.7 GHz, supported by 32.0 GB of RAM and 472 GB of local storage. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, alongside strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations generally align with average tier benchmarks. While the instance delivers strong text generation speeds for small language models, it exhibits weaker prompt processing speeds on medium-sized models. This server offers a balanced resource profile, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for general-purpose applications, web serving, text processing, and database workloads that do not require massive multi-core scaling or dedicated GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8ads_v6 is an 8-vCPU general-purpose VM featuring an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 32 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and text-processing performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8ads_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage, optimized for single-core workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated compute, integrated local storage, and exceptional single-core processing performance for balanced workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz.
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and 472 GB of local storage.
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance.
Features AVX-512 vector extensions and hardware-accelerated cryptography.
Best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and database workloads.
